title: Last chance to buy a PlayStation 5 Digital Edition for $399 and save $200 before Sony's price hikes secure your savings today before MSRP skyrockets from $499 to $599 on April 2
summary: GameStop is offering the PlayStation 5 Slim Digital Edition for $399.99, a $100 discount from its MSRP of $499.99. This deal is available before the price is expected to increase to $599.99 on April 2.
insight: Save $100 on PS5 Slim Digital Edition before MSRP hike.

title: AMD Zen 6 'Venice' ES chips break cover with up to 192 cores, 32 per CCD, in early stress test Kenya, Congo, Nigeria platforms leaked
summary: AMD's Zen 6 'Venice' engineering samples were discovered with up to 192 cores. The samples feature 32 cores per Chiplet Die Design (CCD). This suggests a significant increase in core density.
insight: Massive core density boost for future AMD server processors.

title: Revolut Bank ordered to hand over details of more than 300 Sky TV subscribers connected to piracy operation TV provider expected to pursue subscribers who paid for illicit 'IPTV is Easy' service in unprecedented move
summary: Ireland's High Court ordered Revolut Bank to hand over details of 304 Sky TV subscribers connected to a piracy operation. The order is aimed at pursuing those who paid for an illicit 'IPTV is Easy' service. Sky will receive names, addresses, and banking details from the bank.
insight: Pursuing pirates in the digital age gets a boost in transparency

title: TSMC industrial espionage saga heading to verdict next month in unprecedented Taiwan National Security Act case former engineer accused of stealing 2nm technical info, faces a total of up to 20 years in prison if found guilty
summary: A former TSMC engineer faces verdict for stealing 2nm technical info, potentially up to 20 years in prison if found guilty. The case is one of three landmark cases involving trade secret theft from TSMC. A verdict is scheduled for April 27.
insight: Taiwan National Security Act implications for trade secret protection worldwide matter

title: Analyzing Elon Musk's TeraFab A step towards Tesla and SpaceX's partial vertical integration, or an unattainable dream?
summary: Elon Musk's TeraFab aims to manufacture terawatts of semiconductors, supporting Tesla and SpaceX's vertical integration goals. However, scaling this massive project remains uncertain. The venture's success will impact the companies' reliance on external chip suppliers.
insight: TeraFab's success hinges on solving complex semiconductor manufacturing challenges.

title: Latest Windows 11 update is broken, refuses to install Microsoft pulls latest update over missing files error
summary: The latest Windows 11 update has been pulled due to a missing file error, preventing installation for many users. This update aimed to bring production-quality improvements, particularly in AI-related issues. There is no readily available workaround as of now.
insight: Windows 11 update failure exposes Microsoft's ongoing quality control issues.

title: Chinese chip industry leaders admit the country lags five to ten years behind in AI data center chips AI demand is straining equipment and talent supply
summary: Chinese chip industry leaders acknowledge a significant gap in AI data center chips, with AI demand straining equipment and talent supply.
insight: China lags behind in AI data center chip technology development.

title: Asus ROG Strix Morph 96 Wireless Review: Cheaper, but not really?
summary: The Asus ROG Strix Morph 96 Wireless offers a compact keyboard with great build and battery life, however, its price may not be as competitive as initially thought.
insight: Older, pricier sibling is cheaper, making new model's value uncertain.

title: Dell UltraSharp U3226Q 4K 32-inch QD-OLED monitor review: Pro-grade with reference-level color
summary: The Dell UltraSharp U3226Q is a 32-inch QD-OLED monitor with 4K resolution, delivering reference-level color and industry-standard color modes. It features a high refresh rate, adaptive sync, and multiple HDR formats. The built-in calibrator ensures precise color accuracy.
insight: Professional-grade color accuracy for high-end content creation and editing

title: Dev showcases seamless, massive world with zero loading screens on N64 hardware 30-year-old Nintendo retro console coaxed into draw distances matching the scale of Skyrim
summary: A developer has created an open-world engine on original Nintendo 64 hardware, showcasing massive worlds with zero loading screens. This achievement pushes the limits of what was thought possible on the 30-year-old retro console. The engine rivals the draw distances of the iconic game Skyrim.
insight: Revolutionizing retro console gaming with unprecedented open-world capabilities.

title: Just $151 for 32GB of Corsair Vengeance DDR5-6400 RAM when bundled with Intel's new 270K-Plus and Z890-E motherboard start your Arrow Lake refresh build for less than $800
summary: Upgrade to a powerful Arrow Lake build with 32GB of DDR5 RAM, Intel's 270K-Plus CPU, and Asus' ROG Strix Z890-E motherboard for under $800.
insight: Affordable high-performance build options emerge with Intel's Arrow Lake refresh.

title: Xbox 360 devkit bought for $5 at car boot sale came with 2007 beta build of GTA IV with unreleased assets Version includes cut ferry system, zombies, and more
summary: A pre-release 2007 build of GTA IV was discovered on an Xbox 360 devkit. The build includes unused assets such as the ferry system, zombies, NPCs, and vehicles. This suggests significant changes were made during the game's development.
insight: Rare glimpse into GTA IV's pre-release development and scrapped features

title: Rockchip RK3506J-based Forlinx FCU1501 fanless industrial IoT gateway offers dual Ethernet, plenty of I/Os and serial interfaces
summary: Forlinx Technology introduces the FCU1501, a rugged, fanless industrial IoT gateway with Rockchip RK3506J processor. The system supports high-reliability data acquisition, protocol conversion, and operates in harsh environments. It features dual Ethernet, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and 4G LTE Cat 1 support.
insight: Rugged, high-reliability IoT gateway for harsh industrial environments

title: T-Display-P4 smartphone-like devkit features ESP32-P4 MCU, ESP32-C6 wireless SoC, and SX1262/LR2021 LoRa transceiver
summary: The LILYGO T-Display-P4 is a feature-rich smartphone-like devkit with ESP32-P4 and ESP32-C6 connectivity, GPS, and LoRaWAN support, featuring a range of sensors, displays, and expansion options.
insight: Ruggedized ESP32-P4 devkit for IoT and industrial applications expands connectivity options.

title: Artemis II launch planned for Wednesday with four crew, sensors
summary: The Artemis II mission is a 10-day trip orbiting the Moon with four crew members on board, marking a new distance record for humans in space. The mission aims to test the systems and capabilities of the Orion spacecraft. This historic flight will prepare for future lunar missions.
insight: Breaking distance records in space exploration for human safety and progress.

title: Seventh group of Rising Stars named
summary: The seventh group of Fierce Sensors Rising Stars was named for 2026. The honorees will be recognized at Sensors Converge in May. This group represents emerging talent in the industry.
insight: Emerging talent in sensors technology drives innovation and progress.

title: Tire sensors in cars can be used to track you
summary: Researchers at IMDEA Networks found that tire sensor signals can be easily collected, revealing a hidden privacy risk. This data can potentially be used to track individuals, compromising their location and movements. The study emphasizes the need for better data protection.
insight: Tire sensors can be used to track your every move.

title: Doom now runs over DNS, loading from nearly 2,000 text records
summary: A port of Doom loads into memory via DNS TXT records without writing to disk. The project encodes the game into 1,964 DNS records, utilizing Cloudflare. This allows the game to boot via a PowerShell command.
insight: DNS TXT records used for game loading challenge traditional storage methods.

title: Kingdom Come: Deliverance II studio axes human translator in favor of AI
summary: Warhorse Studios is switching to AI-based translations for future games, sparking fan discontent.
insight: Loss of human touch in game translation may compromise cultural authenticity.

title: Price spike hits Intel Core Ultra 7 270K Plus a week after launch, but value proposition remains
summary: The Intel Core Ultra 7 270K Plus experiences a price spike just after launch, with prices exceeding its MSRP on various online retailers. Despite this, its value proposition remains intact for many users. The price increase is attributed to high demand.
insight: High demand drives price spike, despite Intel Core's strong value proposition.

title: Hackers are using fake coding jobs to spread malware through GitHub
summary: Hackers are using fake coding jobs to spread malware through GitHub, which uses public blockchains as part of its delivery system.
insight: Malware leverages public blockchains for delivery and data exfiltration.

title: It was 1995, 15-inch 800x600 monitors were the norm, what kind of monitor did John Carmack use to code Quake?
summary: In 1995, John Carmack likely used a high-end S3 ViRGE or ATI 3D Rage monitor to code Quake, which featured a 15-inch 800x600 display, a significant upgrade from the norm at that time.
insight: High-end hardware fueled innovation in early 3D game development.

title: Robots just installed 100 MW of solar panels in the desert
summary: Robots have greatly increased solar panel installation efficiency and speed in the desert, enabling 100 MW of solar panels to be installed.
insight: Robot-assisted installation boosts solar energy deployment at unprecedented rates.

title: TSMC's 3nm capacity is now reserved for its biggest customers only
summary: TSMC's 3nm capacity is limited, forcing it to prioritize loyal clients. Demand far exceeds supply, with many companies seeking access to the Taiwanese foundry's advanced nodes. This bottleneck affects the entire chip industry.
insight: Limited supply outstrips demand, prioritizing loyal clients over others

title: MSI's 200Hz gaming monitor drops to $85, undercutting most budget displays
summary: The MSI MAG 242F budget gaming monitor is now available for $85, offering a 200Hz refresh rate, 0.5ms response time, and adaptive sync for smooth gameplay.
insight: High-performance gaming at an affordable price becomes increasingly accessible.

title: Vivo X300 Ultra takes aim at DSLRs with Zeiss lenses and pro photography tools
summary: Vivo's X300 Ultra smartphone features advanced imaging hardware with Zeiss lenses, targeting DSLR territory.
insight: Smartphone makers blur lines between mobile and professional photography capabilities.

title: CachyOS emerges as a fast, gaming-ready Arch Linux OS alternative
summary: CachyOS is a fast, gaming-ready Arch Linux distribution with a polished experience.
insight: CachyOS offers gamers cutting-edge software without sacrificing responsiveness or control.

title: Amazon is spending $4 billion to bring next-day delivery service to rural America
summary: Amazon is investing $4 billion in next-day delivery for rural Americans, targeting a significant market of $1 trillion in annual retail purchases.
insight: Rural residents spend 20% of US retail purchases annually.

title: You can buy a $10,000 iPhone 17 Pro with a piece of Steve Jobs' turtleneck embedded inside
summary: Caviar is releasing a $10,000 iPhone 17 Pro with Steve Jobs' turtleneck material embedded. The device, called the 50 Anniversary Edition, coincides with Apple's 50th anniversary. It features a unique and exclusive design element.
insight: Extravagant tech items highlight luxury market's growing interest in exclusivity

title: Microsoft plans 100% native Windows 11 apps in major shift away from web wrappers
summary: Microsoft plans to create 100% native Windows 11 apps, shifting away from web wrappers. A new team will work on building these apps, with a focus on strong product thinking. This change aims to improve user experience and app performance.
insight: Native apps improve user experience and app performance significantly always

title: ChatGPT isn't getting spicier OpenAI puts adult mode on indefinite hold
summary: OpenAI has put ChatGPT's adult mode on indefinite hold, differing from Grok's approach.
insight: OpenAI prioritizes family-friendly content over adult-oriented chatbot features.

title: New magnetic field system enables wireless communication 100 meters underground
summary: A new magnetic field system enables wireless communication 100 meters underground by leveraging low-frequency magnetic fields that can propagate through solid ground with less attenuation than conventional radio waves.
insight: Magnetic induction overcomes radio wave attenuation underground challenges.

title: Audiophile myth challenged as $4,000 boutique audio cable fails to beat $7 Amazon Basics in lab test
summary: Audiophile myth is challenged as a $4,000 cable fails to outperform a $7 Amazon Basics cable in lab tests, highlighting the limitations of high-end audio equipment.
insight: Expensive audio equipment may not guarantee superior audio quality.
